title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: covariates regressed full dataset cross-validation, held-test information leaks training pipeline. can inflate distort --sample performance. cpmr, leakage-safe behavior means: fit covariate regression fold’s training split ; apply learned regression parameters train test data inside fold; keep parameter-learning steps (example scaling model selection) inside CV well.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
code: library(cpmr)  set.seed(123) n <- 80 p <- 120  # Synthetic connectivity matrix (rows = subjects, cols = edges) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(n * p), nrow = n, ncol = p)  # A synthetic covariate (for example age-like nuisance variable) covariates <- matrix(rnorm(n), ncol = 1)  # Build behavior with both signal and covariate effects edge_signal <- rowMeans(conmat[, 1:10, drop = FALSE]) behav <- 0.7 * edge_signal + 0.6 * covariates[, 1] + rnorm(n, sd = 0.5)  # Leakage-safe CV call: covariates are handled fold-wise inside resampling fit <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  collect_metrics(fit) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>    fold n_assess    both     pos    neg #>   <int>    <int>   <dbl>   <dbl>  <dbl> #> 1     1       16  0.142   0.142  NA     #> 2     2       16  0.447   0.447  NA     #> 3     3       16  0.163   0.294  -0.270 #> 4     4       16  0.694   0.694  NA     #> 5     5       16 -0.0624 -0.0624 NA

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Use fit(cpm_spec(...), ...) single fit fit_resamples() CV.
code: single_fit <- fit(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   return_edges = "sum" )  resamples_fit <- fit_resamples(   cpm_spec(),   conmat = conmat,   behav = behav,   covariates = covariates,   kfolds = 5 )  collect_metrics(resamples_fit) #> # A tibble: 5 × 5 #>    fold n_assess   both    pos     neg #>   <int>    <int>  <dbl>  <dbl>   <dbl> #> 1     1       16  0.273  0.361 -0.195  #> 2     2       16 -0.219 -0.202 -0.0348 #> 3     3       16  0.330  0.330 NA      #> 4     4       16  0.502  0.502 NA      #> 5     5       16  0.182  0.182 NA collect_predictions(resamples_fit) #> # A tibble: 80 × 6 #>      row  fold     real    both     pos       neg #>    <int> <int>    <dbl>   <dbl>   <dbl>     <dbl> #>  1     1     4 -0.586   -0.137  -0.137   1.65e-17 #>  2     2     4  0.726   -0.0626 -0.0626  1.65e-17 #>  3     3     2  0.478    0.112   0.0806  6.62e- 2 #>  4     4     3  0.426    0.147   0.147  -1.73e-17 #>  5     5     5 -1.53    -0.0774 -0.0774 -1.04e-17 #>  6     6     3  0.500    0.568   0.568  -1.73e-17 #>  7     7     5 -0.272   -0.0547 -0.0547 -1.04e-17 #>  8     8     4 -0.00964 -0.238  -0.238   1.65e-17 #>  9     9     5  0.201    0.0220  0.0220 -1.04e-17 #> 10    10     3  0.556    0.150   0.150  -1.73e-17 #> # ℹ 70 more rows collect_edges(resamples_fit, format = "index") #> NULL

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: large feature spaces, fold-wise edge storage can grow quickly. Use return_edges = "sum" keep fold-aggregated edge counts. Use collect_edges(..., format = "index") export sparse indices. Use return_edges = "none" need predictive performance.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Use checklist building CPM workflows: Split train/test folds parameter-learning step. Fit covariate regression training split . Apply training-fitted covariate regression fold’s test split. Keep scaling feature selection inside fold. comparing multiple settings, use nested CV model selection. Report CV design covariate strategy explicitly.

title: Leakage-Safe Covariate Handling in CPM
text: Snoek L, Miletic S, Scholte HS (2019). control confounds decoding analyses neuroimaging data. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2018.09.074 Scheinost D, Noble S, Horien C, et al. (2019). Ten simple rules predictive modeling individual differences neuroimaging. NeuroImage. https://doi.org/10.1016/j.neuroimage.2019.02.057 Rosenblatt M, Tejavibulya L, Jiang R, Noble S, Scheinost D (2024). Data leakage inflates prediction performance connectome-based machine learning models. Nature Communications. https://doi.org/10.1038/s41467-024-46150-w

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: cpmr package specifically designed analysis connectome predictive modeling (CPM) method R. package relies Rfast row oriented calculation.

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: can install released version cpmr CRAN : can install development version cpmr r-universe :

title: Connectome Predictive Modelling in R
text: simple use package. Just shape connectivity matrix subjects edges matrix, .e., row contains correlation matrix (removed diagonal duplicated values, e.g., lower triangular data) subject, behavior data vector feed fit().
code: library(cpmr)  withr::local_seed(123) conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 1000), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100) spec <- cpm_spec() res <- fit(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav, return_edges = "sum") res #> CPM results: #>   Call: fit(object = spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav, return_edges = "sum") #>   Number of observations: 100 #>     Complete cases: 100 #>   Number of edges: 1000 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       FALSE #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.01 #>     Stored splits:    1 #>     Bias correction:  TRUE summary(res) #> CPM summary: #>   Performance (Pearson): #>     Positive: 0.595 #>     Negative: 0.387 #>     Combined: 0.676 #>   Prop. edges (50% folds): #>     Positive: 0.70% #>     Negative: 0.20%

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object returned fit() fitting cpm_spec() single dataset.

title: cpm Fitted Object — cpm
text: cpm object list following elements: call Matched call used fitting. spec originating cpm_spec object. model Trained CPM model components used prediction. real Observed behavior values preprocessing decisions. pred Matrix -sample predictions (, pos, neg). edges Stored edge selection output according return_edges. folds List row indices used fitting. single-fit, contains one element included cases. params Parameter list used fit time.

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object returned fit_resamples() stores fold-level outputs resampling.

title: cpm_resamples Resampling Object — cpm_resamples
text: cpm_resamples object list following elements: spec originating cpm_spec object. folds List assessment-row indices fold. metrics Data frame fold-level performance metrics. predictions Data frame observation-level predictions fold IDs. edges Stored edge output based return_edges (NULL/matrix/array). params Parameter list used resampling run.

what: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: Create lightweight specification object stores modeling parameters required fit connectome-based predictive model later fit().

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: cpm_spec(   thresh_method = c("alpha", "sparsity"),   thresh_level = 0.01,   bias_correct = TRUE )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   return_edges = c("sum", "none", "all"),   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )  # S3 method for class 'cpm_spec' fit_resamples(   object,   conmat,   behav,   ...,   covariates = NULL,   resamples = NULL,   kfolds = NULL,   return_edges = c("none", "sum", "all"),   na_action = c("fail", "exclude") )

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: thresh_method, thresh_level threshold method level used edge selection. method set "alpha", edge selection based critical value correlation coefficient. method set "sparsity", edge selection based quantile correlation coefficient, thus network sparsity controlled. bias_correct Logical value indicating connectome data bias-corrected. TRUE, connectome data centered scaled unit variance based training data model fitting prediction. See Rapuano et al. (2020) details. object cpm_spec object. conmat matrix connectome data. Observations row, edges column (assumed duplicated edges removed). behav numeric vector contains behavior data. Length must equal number observations conmat. Note behav also row/column matrix, converted vector using drop(). ... future extension. Currently ignored. covariates matrix covariates. Observations row, variables column. NULL, covariates used. Note vector provided, converted column matrix. return_edges character string indicating return value selected edges. "none", edges returned/stored. "sum", edge masks returned single-fit summed across folds resampling. "", single-fit stores 3D array singleton third dimension resampling stores fold-wise edge arrays. na_action character string indicating action missing values found behav. "fail", error thrown. "exclude", missing values excluded analysis kept output. resamples Optional list assessment indices defining resamples. element must integer vector indexing rows conmat. NULL, folds generated kfolds. kfolds Number folds used resamples NULL. NULL, set number complete-case observations (LOOCV).

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
text: cpm_spec object storing parameters later fitting. fitted cpm object single -sample fit. cpm_resamples object containing fold-level metrics observation-level predictions.

title: Define a CPM model specification — cpm_spec
code: spec <- cpm_spec(thresh_level = 0.01) spec #> CPM model specification: #>   Threshold method: alpha #>   Threshold level:  0.01 #>   Bias correction:  TRUE  conmat <- matrix(rnorm(100 * 100), nrow = 100) behav <- rnorm(100) fit(spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav) #> CPM results: #>   Call: fit(object = spec, conmat = conmat, behav = behav) #>   Number of observations: 100 #>     Complete cases: 100 #>   Number of edges: 100 #>   Parameters: #>     Covariates:       FALSE #>     Threshold method: alpha #>     Threshold level:  0.01 #>     Stored splits:    1 #>     Bias correction:  TRUE

title: Summary of a cpm object. — summary.cpm
text: object object class cpm. ... parameters passed function. method character vector indicating method used calculate correlation real predicted values. edge_level numeric value 0 1 indicating proportional threshold edge selection. x object class cpm_summary.

title: Summary of a cpm object. — summary.cpm
text: list class cpm_summary containing two elements: performance matrix prediction performance, including correlation real predicted values edges, positive edges , negative edges . edges logical vector indicating whether edge selected based edge_level. params list parameters used summary.

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: Tidy cpm object

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
code: # S3 method for class 'cpm' tidy(x, ..., component = c("performance", "edges"))

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: x cpm object. ... Additional arguments passed summary(). component character vector indicating component tidy.

title: Tidy a cpm object — tidy.cpm
text: tibble columns storing parameters cpm object columns depending component argument: component = "performance": method method used calculate correlation real predicted values. pos correlation real predicted values positive edges. neg correlation real predicted values negative edges. component = "edges": level proportional threshold edge selection. pos logical vector indicating whether edge selected based edge_level (positive). neg logical vector indicating whether edge selected based edge_level (negative).

title: cpmr (development version)
text: cpm() removed. legacy entry-point function cpm() deleted. Use fit(cpm_spec(...), conmat, behav) instead. confounds parameter (deprecated alias covariates) also removed. fit() now performs single fit. Cross-validation/resampling workflows handled fit_resamples().

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Added cpm_spec() model specification object fit() dispatch primary API connectome-based predictive modeling. print.cpm_spec() provides concise summary modeling parameters. Added cpm_resamples result class dedicated methods: collect_metrics(), collect_predictions(), collect_edges(). Added edge export support via collect_edges(format = "index") sparse index output edge storage large.

title: cpmr (development version)
text: Refactored fit internals focused modules (fit-generics, cpm-spec, cpm-resamples, fit-internals) improve maintainability. Updated expanded tests internal helper branches edge/summary coverage paths. Stabilized GitHub Actions uploads Codecov coverage Test Analytics generating uploading JUnit test results explicit workspace path.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-03-11

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Renamed internal constant networks includes corr_types inc_edges, respectively, better reflect purpose. addition, documentation mentioned networks updated use correlation types better reflect meaning. Reorganized R/cpm.R cpm() remains entry-point function top, followed print.cpm(), internal helpers grouped . Improved test architecture explicit internal-helper coverage complete-case fold invariants.

title: cpmr 0.1.1
text: Refactored internal cpm() workflow smaller helpers (normalize_inputs, resolve_include_cases, apply_confounds_regression, etc.) preserving user-facing behavior. Removed obsolete internal alias regress_counfounds(). Removed broad Rfast namespace import favor explicit Rfast:: calls. Updated summary.cpm() edge summarization path avoid mutating local object fields.

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: CRAN release: 2024-10-06

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added summary() method summarize results CPM analysis (#8). Added tidy() method tidy results CPM analysis (#10). Support na_action argument cpm() function handle missing values input data (#2).

title: cpmr 0.1.0
text: Added params cpm() output store input arguments (#14). Let "sum" default value return_edges argument. Let first two dimensions edges output edges networks, respectively. Polish print method cpm class.
